expectations.text: uses-new-dimensions: Output uses C1-C4/O1-O4/V1-V3 dimensions, not E1-E4
expectations.evidence: The assessment report uses C1-C4 (Controllability), O1-O4 (Observability), and V1-V3 (Value) dimensions throughout. No E1-E4 dimensions appear. Example from output.txt lines 22-46: 'C1 Config Control: 0/1', 'O1 Semantic Logs: 0/1', 'V1 Intrusion Delta: 0/1', etc.

expectations.text: code-injection-not-files: Output proposes code injections with [HARNESS-INJECT-START] markers, not just .harness/ wrapper scripts
expectations.evidence: Code injections use [HARNESS-INJECT-START]/[HARNESS-INJECT-END] markers embedded in source files. Evidence from proposed-patches.md: line 22 has '// [HARNESS-INJECT-START: config_control]', line 53 has '// [HARNESS-INJECT-END: config_control]', line 133 has '// [HARNESS-INJECT-START: structured_log]'. Patch files also contain these markers (patch_1_config_control.patch line 16, patch_2_observability_harness_ts.patch line 53). The patches modify src/bootstrap/state.ts, src/harness.ts (new file), and src/main.tsx - all source files, not just wrapper scripts.

expectations.text: injection-point-analysis: Output identifies specific files and line ranges for injection points
expectations.evidence: Output clearly identifies specific injection points with file paths and line numbers. From output.txt lines 124-128: 'src/utils/config.ts | After line 1817 (EOF) | Add new functions', 'src/utils/config.ts | Lines 1713, 1739 | Patch existing reads'. From lines 136-139: 'src/utils/debug.ts | After line 226 | Add new functions', 'src/utils/debug.ts | Lines 203-228 | Patch to emit JSON when HARNESS_CONTROL=1'. Each patch file also specifies exact locations: patch_1_config_control.patch specifies 'After line 25 (after imports, before first export)', patch_2_observability_harness_ts.patch specifies 'NEW FILE', patch_3_main_init.patch specifies 'After line 95'.

expectations.text: shows-approval-flow: Output shows patches before applying (PROPOSE + APPROVE phases)
expectations.evidence: Output explicitly uses PROPOSE + APPROVE workflow terminology. From proposed-patches.md section 3 header: 'PROPOSED PATCHES (Pending Approval)'. Patch files include 'Status: PENDING APPROVAL' at top. Output.txt section 4 line 93: 'SECTION 4: PROPOSED PATCHES'. The report presents patches as proposals awaiting approval, not yet applied. All patches show 'Status: PENDING APPROVAL' and 'Activation: HARNESS_CONTROL=1' indicating conditional, not-yet-applied changes.

expectations.text: tier-recommendation: Output recommends a specific starting tier (0-5)
expectations.evidence: Output recommends a specific tier combination. From output.txt lines 100-102: 'Recommended Starting Point: Tier 1 (Config Control) + Tier 2 (Observability)'. Lines 111-116 provide escalation path: 'Tier 1+2 pass -> Tier 3 (Feature Flags)', 'Tier 3 pass -> Tier 4 (State Injection)', 'Tier 4 pass -> Tier 5 (Full Control Plane)'. The assessment report section 5 line 147: 'Tier Recommendation: Tier 1 (Config Control) + Tier 2 (Observability)'.
